revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Housono-jinja]][[ja:祝園神社]] {{Expand Japanese|祝園神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Housono Shrine | native_name = 祝園神社 | image = Hosono Shrine2.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Housono Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.768023|135.801568}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|eo|Takemikazuĉi|es|Takemikazuchi|fa|تاکه‌میکازوچی|fr|Takemikazuchi|id|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|pl|Takemikazuchi|pt|Takemikadzuchi|ru|Такэмикадзути|zh|建御雷|qid=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Housono Shrine'''|祝園神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamashiro Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135009132|ltq=Q135009132|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|lt=Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|simple|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11467693|ltq=Q11467693|qid=Q11467693|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province}} ** Series ordinal: 85 ** P155: {{ill|Chigi Shrine|lt=Chigi Shrine|qid=Q135038809|1=Chigi Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135038810|ltq=Q135038810|qid=Q135038810|1=Wakinoni- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1203237|ltq=Q1203237|qid=Q1203237|1=Seika, Kyoto}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|lt=Yamashiro Province|cs|Provincie Jamaširo|de|Provinz Yamashiro|en|Yamashiro Province|es|Provincia de Yamashiro|fa|ولایت یاماشیرو|fr|Province de Yamashiro|gl|Provincia de Yamashiro|hak|Yamashiro-koet|id|Provinsi Yamashiro|it|Yamashiro (provincia)|ja|山城国|ko|야마시로국|lt|Jamaširo žemė|ms|Yamashiro Province|nl|Yamashiro (provincie)|pt|Província de Yamashiro|ru|Ямасиро (провинция)|simple|Yamashiro Province|sk|Jamaširo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Јамаширо|tr|Yamashiro (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ямашіро|wuu|山城国|zh_min_nan|Yamasiro-kok|zh_yue|山城國|zh|山城國|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} * {{ill|Q1154661|ltq=Q1154661|qid=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.768023|135.801568}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hosono Shrine2.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q1502690|ltq=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041120195730/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=12001 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180654 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ほうそのじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5130005008616 == Commons category (P373) == * Housono-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '日本・京都府相楽郡精華町大字祝園小字柞ノ森（ほほそのもり）18番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '京都府相楽郡精華町祝園', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 祝園神社 {{commons category|Category:Housono-jinja}} [[JA:祝園神社]] {{wikidata link|Q19841356}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Housono Shrine |画像= [[File:Hosono Shrine2.JPG|frameless|260px]]
Torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|qid=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Seika|Q1203237|qid=Q1203237|1=Seika, Kyoto}} Ōaza Housono, Koaza Hoho-sonomori 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |神事= {{ill|lt=Igomori Festival|Q22123234|qid=Q22123234|1=Igomori Festival}} (3 days each year from the Kōshin day of {{ill|lt=New Year|Q701398|qid=Q701398|1=Japanese New Year}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Housono Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Japanese crest Sagari Fuji.svg|thumb|right|250px|Shrine crest of Housono Shrine.]] '''Housono Shrine''' (ほうそのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seika|Q1203237|qid=Q1203237|1=Seika, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|qid=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is enshrined at the place where {{ill|lt=Takehaniyasuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11545865|qid=Q11545865|1=Prince Takehaniyasuhiko}}, said to have rebelled in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was reportedly defeated''National Shrine Directory Vol. 2'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society, {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|qid=Q2481|1=1977}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 52), p.91. Its shrine crest is “Sagari Fuji.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines three deities: {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. === History === The founding period is unknown, but the {{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} (record of shrine households, decree of {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}})) includes “Housono deity, four households, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}},” so it is presumed to have existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} deity register records it as “Housono Shrine”Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture, p.107. === Rituals === Every year for three days from the {{ill|lt=Kōshin|Q1062011|qid=Q1062011|1=Yang Wood Monkey}} day of {{ill|lt=New Year|Q701398|qid=Q701398|1=Japanese New Year}}, the {{ill|lt=Igomori Festival|Q22123234|qid=Q22123234|1=Igomori Festival}} is held. It is based on a {{ill|lt=legend|Q44342|qid=Q44342|1=Legend}} that in the era of the 10th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, {{ill|lt=Takehaniyasuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11545865|qid=Q11545865|1=Prince Takehaniyasuhiko}} rebelled and was defeated, but became an {{ill|lt=evil spirit|Q177413|qid=Q177413|1=Demon}} and remained in this area ruining fields, so the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=villagers|1=UNKNOWN}} secluded themselves in ritual abstinence to pacify the spirit{{Cite journal |和書|author=Kentarō Tamino |title=Structure and Change of Tug-of-War Rituals: A Case of Seika, Sōraku District, Kyoto |journal=Japan Society of Physical Education Conference Proceedings |publisher=Japan Society of Physical Education |year=1997 |volume=48 |issue=48th Conference (1997) |pages=639 |naid=110001903306 |doi=10.20693/jspeconf.48.0_639 |url=https://doi.org/10.20693/jspeconf.48.0_639}} {{ja icon}}. In old times, during the festival, {{ill|lt=cattle|Q830|qid=Q830|1=Cattle}} and {{ill|lt=horses|Q726|qid=Q726|1=Horse}} were entrusted to neighboring villages, all {{ill|lt=sound|Q11461|qid=Q11461|1=Sound}} was forbidden, and people stayed in seclusion eating only {{ill|lt=shōjin cuisine|Q1326339|qid=Q1326339|1=Buddhist cuisine}}. On day 1, priests perform the “Furoi rite” in darkness; on day 2, the “Mita rite” with torches; and on day 3, the “tug-of-war rite”. The rope is a huge ring about 1 meter in diameter made by wrapping split bamboo with straw and attaching six bamboo poles; {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q767655|qid=Q767655|lt=parishioners|1=Ujigami}} divide into north and south sides and hold three tug-of-war rounds. After the winner is decided, the rope is carried to {{ill|lt=Izumori|Q20043114|qid=Q20043114|1=Izumori}}, said to be the execution site of Takehaniyasuhiko, and burned. === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|qid=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Shin-Hōsono Station|Q7497094|qid=Q7497094|1=Shin-Hōsono Station}}, 20 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|qid=Q1073379|1=Katamachi Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Hōsono Station|Q855574|qid=Q855574|1=Hōsono Station}}, 20 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Kunihiko Shimonaka|Q59533176|qid=Q59533176|1=Kunihiko Shimonaka}} (ed.), ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', Japan Historical Place-Name Series Vol.26 ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}} / {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56) === External links === * [https://www.town.seika.kyoto.jp/kakuka/shogai/1/kannkoubunnka/2/3703.html Housono Shrine (Seika Town, February 20, 2023)] {{ja icon}} * {{Wayback|url=https://blog.goo.ne.jp/pzm4366/e/fce11610d259e50f0baebb034ebf985c |title=Kyoto Seika Town: Housono Shrine Igomori Festival (Day 3 - Tug-of-War Rite) |date=20150123033351}} {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Housono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Housono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Housono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:精華町の建築物]] [[Category:精華町の歴史]]
